```{r, echo=TRUE}
# Here we are exploring behaviors on women's Antenatal–Intrapartum–Postnatal Continuum Journey:
  ## ANC: anc.any (at least 1 ANC), anc.4plus (4 or more ANC visits)
  ## Delivery: fac.deli: this is recoded; for those who said "other" they are NOT counted as facility delivery.
  ## PNC for woman, PNC for baby: wom.hlthck1, baby.hlthck1
  ## Breastfeeding: breastfed.last, breastfed.excl
  ## Vaccination: dpt.full.yn.y (any child fully vacinated with DPT)
  ## mCP current: fp.mod.now
```

```{r}
#senegal$anc.less4
#senegal$wom.nohlthck
senegal$anc.4plus <- ifelse(senegal$anc.less4 == 0, 1, 0)

anc.mean <- as.integer(mean(senegal$m14_1, na.rm=TRUE))
senegal$anc.num <- ifelse(senegal$m14_1 == 98, anc.mean, senegal$m14_1)
senegal$anc.any <- ifelse(senegal$anc.num >0, 1, 0)

anc.1st.mean <- as.integer(mean(senegal$m13_1, na.rm=TRUE))
senegal$anc.1stvisit <- ifelse(senegal$m13_1 == 98, anc.1st.mean, senegal$m13_1)
senegal$anc.1stvisit[senegal$m14_1 == 0] <- 10
senegal$anc.1sttri <- ifelse(senegal$anc.1stvisit < 4, 1, 0)

# table(senegal$anc.4plus, useNA = "always")
# table(senegal$anc.any, useNA = "always")
# table(senegal$anc.1sttri, useNA = "always")

#table(senegal$fac.deli, useNA = "always")

senegal$wom.hlthck1 <- ifelse(senegal$wom.nohlthck==0, 1, 0)
senegal$baby.hlthck1 <- ifelse(senegal$baby.nohlthck==0, 1, 0)
# table(senegal$wom.hlthck1, useNA = "always")
# table(senegal$baby.hlthck1, useNA = "always")
# table(senegal$breastfed.excl, useNA = "always")
# table(senegal$dpt.full.yn.y, useNA = "always")
# table(senegal$fp.mod.now, useNA = "always")
senegal.des <-svydesign(id=~v021, strata=~v023, weights=~wt, data=senegal, nest=TRUE)
```

#### 3.6.1. Prevelance by segment
```{r}
# List of variables
senegal <- senegal %>% 
  mutate(fp.mod.now = ifelse(nofp.mod.now == 0, 1, 0))
senegal.des <-svydesign(id=~v021, strata=~v023, weights=~wt, data=senegal, nest=TRUE)
vars <- c("anc.any", "anc.4plus", "fac.deli", "wom.hlthck1", "baby.hlthck1", "breastfed.excl", "dpt.full.yn.y", "fp.mod.now")

get_prop_by_class <- function(var) {
  df <- svyby(
    formula = as.formula(paste0("~", var)),
    by = ~class,
    design = senegal.des,
    FUN = svymean,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
  df <- df %>%
    dplyr::select(class, all_of(var)) %>%
    rename(prop = all_of(var)) %>%
    mutate(variable = var)
  return(df)
}

results_list <- lapply(vars, get_prop_by_class)
results_df <- bind_rows(results_list)

results_df$variable <- factor(results_df$variable,
  levels = vars,
  labels = c("1+ ANC", "4+ ANC", "Facility Delivery", "PNC Woman", "PNC Baby", "Exclusive\nbreastfeeding", "Any Child\nFull-DPT", "Current mCP"))

ggplot(results_df, aes(x = variable, y = prop * 100, fill = variable)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(prop * 100, 0)), vjust = -0.5, size = 3) + 
  facet_wrap(~class, nrow = 2) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 110)) +  
  labs(y = "Percentage", x = NULL, title = "Antenatal–Intrapartum–Postnatal Continuum Journey by Segment (Senegal 2019)", caption = "Weighted proportions based on 2019DHS Segmentation") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = 7, vjust = 0.5),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank())
```

#### 3.6.2. Prevelance by state
```{r}
vars <- c("anc.any", "anc.4plus", "fac.deli", "wom.hlthck1", "baby.hlthck1", "breastfed.excl", "dpt.full.yn.y", "fp.mod.now")

get_prop_by_class <- function(var) {
  df <- svyby(
    formula = as.formula(paste0("~", var)),
    by = ~v024,
    design = senegal.des,
    FUN = svymean,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
  df <- df %>%
    dplyr::select(v024, all_of(var)) %>%
    rename(prop = all_of(var)) %>%
    mutate(variable = var)
  return(df)
}

results_list <- lapply(vars, get_prop_by_class)
results_df <- bind_rows(results_list)

results_df$variable <- factor(results_df$variable,
  levels = vars,
  labels = c("1+ ANC", "4+ ANC", "Facility Delivery", "PNC Woman", "PNC Baby", "Excl. breastfeeding", "Any Child Full-DPT", "Current mCP"))

results_df <- results_df %>%
  mutate(v024 = str_to_title(v024))
results_df <- results_df %>%
  mutate(v024 = ifelse(v024 == "Thi?S", "Thiès", v024))

ggplot(results_df, aes(x = variable, y = prop * 100, fill = variable)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(prop * 100, 0)), vjust = -0.5, size = 2.3) + 
  facet_wrap(~v024, nrow = 2) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 110)) +  
  labs(y = "Percentage", x = NULL, title = "Antenatal–Intrapartum–Postnatal Continuum Journey by State (Senegal 2019)", caption = "Weighted proportions based on 2019DHS Segmentation") +
  theme_minimal(base_size =12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = 6.5, vjust = 0.5),
        axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7.5),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank(),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 8))
```

#### 3.6.3. For State Matam
```{r}
#table(senegal$v024) #louga #matam
matam.des <- subset(senegal.des, v024 == "matam") 
vars <- c("anc.any", "anc.4plus", "fac.deli", "wom.hlthck1", "baby.hlthck1", "breastfed.excl", "dpt.full.yn.y", "fp.mod.now")
get_prop_by_class <- function(var) {
  df <- svyby(
    formula = as.formula(paste0("~", var)),
    by = ~class,
    design = matam.des,
    FUN = svymean,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
  df <- df %>%
    dplyr::select(class, all_of(var)) %>%
    rename(prop = all_of(var)) %>%
    mutate(variable = var)
  return(df)
}

results_list <- lapply(vars, get_prop_by_class)
results_df <- bind_rows(results_list)

results_df$variable <- factor(results_df$variable,
  levels = vars,
  labels = c("1+ ANC", "4+ ANC", "Facility Delivery", "PNC Woman", "PNC Baby", "Exclusive\nbreastfeeding", "Any Child\nFull-DPT", "Current mCP"))

ggplot(results_df, aes(x = variable, y = prop * 100, fill = variable)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(prop * 100, 0)), vjust = -0.5, size = 3) + 
  facet_wrap(~class, nrow = 2) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 110)) +  
  labs(y = "Percentage", x = NULL, title = "Antenatal–Intrapartum–Postnatal Continuum Journey by Segment (Matam)", caption = "Weighted proportions based on 2019DHS Segmentation") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = 7, vjust = 0.5),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank())
```

#### 3.6.4. For State Louga
```{r}
#table(senegal$v024) #louga #matam
louga.des <- subset(senegal.des, v024 == "louga") 
vars <- c("anc.any", "anc.4plus", "fac.deli", "wom.hlthck1", "baby.hlthck1", "breastfed.excl", "dpt.full.yn.y", "fp.mod.now")
get_prop_by_class <- function(var) {
  df <- svyby(
    formula = as.formula(paste0("~", var)),
    by = ~class,
    design = matam.des,
    FUN = svymean,
    na.rm = TRUE
  )
  df <- df %>%
    dplyr::select(class, all_of(var)) %>%
    rename(prop = all_of(var)) %>%
    mutate(variable = var)
  return(df)
}

results_list <- lapply(vars, get_prop_by_class)
results_df <- bind_rows(results_list)

results_df$variable <- factor(results_df$variable,
  levels = vars,
  labels = c("1+ ANC", "4+ ANC", "Facility Delivery", "PNC Woman", "PNC Baby", "Exclusive\nbreastfeeding", "Any Child\nFull-DPT", "Current mCP"))

ggplot(results_df, aes(x = variable, y = prop * 100, fill = variable)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(prop * 100, 0)), vjust = -0.5, size = 3) + 
  facet_wrap(~class, nrow = 2) +
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 110)) +  
  labs(y = "Percentage", x = NULL, title = "Antenatal–Intrapartum–Postnatal Continuum Journey by Segment (Louga)", caption = "Weighted proportions based on 2019DHS Segmentation") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "none",
        strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
        axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, size = 7, vjust = 0.5),
        plot.title = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.background = element_blank())
```

#### 3.6.5. Health Utilization Index
```{r}
# something for showing healthcare engagement, 1st ANC, 4+ANC, delivery, PNC/baby check up/mother (whatever you think is relevant in these 3) ,Vaccination
```

```{r, echo=TRUE}
# Create an index (aip.index) as following
# first ANC happened during 1st trimester, yes: score 1, no: score 0 (anc.1sttri)
# 4+ ANC, yes: score 1, no: score 0 (anc.4plus)
# facility delivery, yes: score 1, no: score 0 (fac.deli)
# Woman had PNC, yes: score 1, no: score 0 (wom.hlthck1)
# Baby had PNC, yes: score 1, no: score 0 (baby.hlthck1)
# Any full DPT, yes: score 1, no: score 0 (dpt.full.yn.y)
```

```{r}
index.vars <- c("anc.1sttri", "anc.4plus", "fac.deli", "wom.hlthck1", "baby.hlthck1", "dpt.full.yn.y")

senegal <- senegal %>%
  mutate(aip.index = rowSums(dplyr::select(., all_of(index.vars)), na.rm = TRUE),
         aip.index = ifelse(rowSums(!is.na(dplyr::select(., all_of(index.vars)))) == 0, NA, aip.index))
# table(senegal$aip.index, useNA = "always")
senegal.des <-svydesign(id=~v021, strata=~v023, weights=~wt, data=senegal, nest=TRUE)
```

```{r}
# We have decided to leave it out for now - the initial purpose of using a visualization of this index was to showcase the touch-point with health system before getting FP. We found it a bit tricky to use data PNC womena and PNC baby. Given how these 2 variables were coded, it includes PNC received at facility (facility delivery) and at home.
```

### 3.7. Spacing
```{r, echo=TRUE}
# available vars for spacing:
#"recent.short", "any.short.interval","all.short", "any.very.short", "proportion.short""any.optimal", "all.optimal", "mean.interval", "median.interval", "min.interval", "max.interval", "inter.7_17.cnt", "inter.18_23.cnt", "inter.24plus.cnt", "total.intervals", "spacing.vary", "spacing.pattern", "spacing.range"

#Use: all.short, mean.interval, spacing.pattern

# table(senegal$all.short, useNA = "always")
# #table(senegal$mean.interval, useNA = "always")
# table(senegal$spacing.pattern, useNA = "always")
# #table(senegal$total.intervals, useNA = "always")
# Actually, all total.intervals >=1; the 2858 are women who did not have birth in the past 5 years, recode spacing.pattern. But for numeric variables like all.short and mean.interval, we need to keep the NA
senegal$spacing.pattern <- ifelse(is.na(senegal$spacing.pattern), "No birth in last 5 years", senegal$spacing.pattern)
senegal$spacing.pattern <- ifelse(senegal$spacing.pattern == "Single/no interval", "Single interval", senegal$spacing.pattern)
#table(senegal$spacing.pattern, useNA = "always")
senegal.des <-svydesign(id=~v021, strata=~v023, weights=~wt, data=senegal, nest=TRUE)

# There are very few are "all short", only 341 out of ~ 5000 who had birth in the past 5 years
```

#### 3.7. Bar of mean.interval of CI
```{r}
spacing_data <- senegal %>% filter(!is.na(mean.interval))
spacing.des <- svydesign(id=~v021, strata=~v023, weights=~wt, data=spacing_data, nest=TRUE)

segment_means <- svyby(~mean.interval, ~class, spacing.des, svymean, na.rm = TRUE)
ggplot(segment_means, aes(x = class, y = mean.interval)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#4a90a4", alpha = 0.7, width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = mean.interval - 1.96*se, ymax = mean.interval + 1.96*se), 
                width = 0.1, color = "red", size = 0.5) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(mean.interval, 1)), 
            vjust = -1.5, size = 3.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Survey-weighted Average Birth Intervals by Segment",
    x = "Segment",
    y = "Mean Birth Interval (months)",
    caption = "Error bars show 95% confidence intervals\nBased on women with births in last 5 years"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.1))) +  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank())
```

#### 3.7.2. Stacked bar
```{r}
spacing_props <- senegal %>%
  filter(!is.na(spacing.pattern)) %>%
  group_by(class, spacing.pattern) %>%
  summarise(weight_sum = sum(wt, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = 'drop') %>%
  group_by(class) %>%
  mutate(
    total_weight = sum(weight_sum),
    proportion = weight_sum / total_weight,
    percentage = round(proportion * 100, 0))

mean_intervals_by_segment <- spacing_data %>%
  group_by(class) %>%
  summarise(
    weighted_mean = weighted.mean(mean.interval, wt, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = 'drop')

p1 <- ggplot(spacing_props, aes(x = class, y = proportion, fill = spacing.pattern)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "stack", width = 0.7) +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(percentage, "%")), 
            position = position_stack(vjust = 0.5), 
            size = 3, color = "white") +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::percent, name = "Proportion") +
  scale_fill_manual(name = "Spacing Pattern",
    values = c(
      "All adequate spacing" = "#4682B4",       
      "All short spacing" = "#DC143C",          
      "Mixed spacing" = "#b590bf",              
      "Single interval" = "#2E8B57",
      "No birth in last 5 years" = "#778899")) +
  labs(
    title = "Birth Spacing Patterns and Mean Intervals by Segment",
    subtitle = "Stacked bars show spacing patterns; red line shows mean birth interval",
    x = "Segment"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 0, hjust = 0.5),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.spacing.x = unit(0.1, 'cm'), 
    legend.spacing.y = unit(0.1, 'cm'),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 6),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 7),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank())

# Add second y-axis for mean intervals
p2 <- p1 + 
  geom_line(data = mean_intervals_by_segment, 
            aes(x = class, y = weighted_mean/100, group = 1), 
            color = "red", size = 1.2, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  geom_point(data = mean_intervals_by_segment, 
             aes(x = class, y = weighted_mean/100), 
             color = "red", size = 3, inherit.aes = FALSE) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::percent, 
    name = "Proportion",
    sec.axis = sec_axis(~.*100, name = "Mean Birth Interval (months)")) +
  labs(caption = "Survey-weighted analysis\nRed line shows mean birth interval (right axis)")

print(p2)
```
